name: Pad Thai (1)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Thai flat rice noodles (bahn pho), OR rice sticks
1/2 lb Fresh firm bean curd, cut into tiny cubes
1/2 lb Chicken breast meat, very thinly sliced
1/4 lb Shrimp, peeled and cut in half the long way
3 tbsp Finely chopped peanuts (dry-roasted salted type)
2 tsp Red chili paste with garlic
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the noodles in ample warm water until supple, about 15 minutes, drain and set aside.
2. Place the noodles in boiling water and cook just until the water returns to a boil. Drain again.
3. Heat oil for deep-frying to 375°F (190°C) and deep-fry cut bean curd. Pat the bean curd dry on a paper towel first so it will not spatter fat. Drain the bean curd and set aside.
4. Heat a large wok and add peanut oil, garlic, and chicken. Stir fry for a few minutes.
5. Add shrimp, drained noodles, beaten eggs, and deep-fried bean curd to the wok. Toss well and stir fry for 3 to 4 minutes over medium-high heat.
6. Add remaining ingredients (except garnishes) and stir or cook for a few more minutes until noodles are hot and tender.
7. Serve on a platter with garnishes, which are an integral part of the dish.
